text: The customer service lately has been a nightmare!! I called because im interested in upgrading my plan into a newer better plan. The deal is if you have 3 lines and add 1 more line (4 line is free) so total for all 4 lines is 185. I currently have 4 lines so because im not technically bringing in a new line I dont qualify, however if I still want to upgrade to this plan I would have to pay 220 for the same plan but someone with 3 lines adding 1 line (free line = 4 in total). How come it doesnt work for someone with 4 lines already. So anyways, I tried to see if I can add another line (5 in total). And use the 5th line that is supposedly going to free through the promotion and use that line with a watch.. apparently this promotion is for phones only. So watches and phones now use different plans??? This sounds all absurd and it seems like they only care about taking care of customer that are bringing in 1 new line. This customer that has had 4 lines for close to 8 years is paying more money than one that has 3 lines with the 1 extra line free. Tmobile rather lose 4 lines but gain 1 I line. Im taking my business elsewhere. TMOBILE has become a real POS with everything including service.

T-Mobile a horrible experience. Horrible customer service inside the store with 
A employee name, Noel I thought he was great until I found out he told me half the truth and majority of a lie  I even thought I paid for a phone come to find out the phone was never paid for it was a down payment he even told me my bill will be 132. The bill was 176. They don’t care because it doesn’t come out of their pocket they’re just trying to make a sale, he felt the need to be dishonest just horrible all the way around. But I will say the assistant manager Michael tried his best to help. He was awesome. But the damage was already done like you’re stuck with it.

In February 2023 we found a promotion that promised a free line if we already had 3 lines and plan Magenta Max, we met those conditions. We called a T-mobile operator to be sure that the fourth line would be for free. The operator confirmed that and promised a total bill would be $170. After the confirmation we ordered a SIM. When we got a SIM, we unfortunately, realized we are charged $200. We called T-mobile again to solve the issue because we were expecting the promotion. They said various reasons why we cannot expect the promotion but after careful review eventually agreed that we are legitimate for the it and the promotion just wasn’t applied in the system. Finally, the operators of T-mobile claim they are not able to help us resolve the problem, and the promotion which was promised by the carrier is not working. We are really dissapointed by this case.

Horrible experience 
Non of the workers wants to help 
I have an account with 8 lines trying to port new number 
No one wants to help 
Very disappointed 
Location 
8686 Hunters Village Rd, Tampa, FL 33647

I initiated a business account with tmobile. From day 1 its been a stressful mess to get the bill they quoted in writing. My first month I was charged twice in a 44 day period. My bill has always been close to $100 more than they quoted and I'm not receiving even half of the perks such as paramount and Facebook advertising that are supposed to be included. I was given a personal business manager but that's a joke. He says he'll fix it and doesn't. If I contact him again he'll refer me to customer service. When I call customer service they assure they'll add a 20% discount but never do. When I go into to store I initiated service with and speak to the store manager, they'll just refer me to my "personal business manager" I've now been with them a little over 3 months and am just over it. I shouldn't have to fight for something I was quoted in writing. I don't see how it's even legal for them to over charge like this.
Service is horrible,  60% of the time,  my phone has no service,  unless there is wifi nearby,  but even that is spotty.

T-mobile Sucks
You want a Bad experience? Call them. They tell you that you will get new equipment and then send Old equipment 
On top of that, try to dispute. Worst people I have dealt with

Had two calls over a rebate for a total of over 1.5 hours - because the link of prepaid matercard they sent to my email is probably made not to function
And I still don't have the rebate
You can't even write to them, what to talk of sending images to show the issue. The customer service is clueless
They have like 3 account numbers for one home internet service but a/c isn't connected to telephone # either

I moved out of the US for work two and a half years ago. I wanted an American telephone provider. T-Mobile (Ellensburg branch) promised me a plan for 100 dollars per month while I was overseas. The service initially worked, but two weeks after I arrived at my job, T-Mobile called me to inform me that they did not support sustained international service, and that my plan would be cancelled. However, they continued to bill me. After numerous calls to them (very expensive to me) as well as chat (which fortunately, I archived) I was able to get them to refund the money they had charged me for a service they weren't providing. They also promised me they would stop billing me. THEN, a few weeks ago, a letter was sent to my aunt in the US from a collection agency trying to collect money on behalf of T-Mobile. I again chatted with a representative who promised me that I would be contacted by email with a resolution withing 72 hours. It's been five days. No response. These people are crooks. I am supposed to write the 'date of experience below.' The dates of the experience are October 2020 until present.

I just left T-mobile. My dad was the account owner I was under a family plan. I would pay my share. Eventually dad left to go to Spectrum because they were wayyyyy better. He kept telling me to get off of T-Mobile and come over to spectrum. I did it today. But when trying to leave T mobile, it’s a maze. They wouldn’t let me log into the account to remove my name. It was all under my dad’s name. Their code wouldn’t work for my dad either and he was in another state. So the guy in the store said to remove you from the account, you will have to call the #. So I called immediately. On the phone they said I have to go to the store. I said I’m right here at it. They said “Are you the account owner?” I’m like “sure I’m the only one on here, paying for this line.” Eventually she says “you’re not so and so.” I’m like so and so left a year ago with the account to me. Same family plan last name. She started immediately accusing me of fraud. Then my dad happen to call right then and it became a 3 way phone call while also in the store. She continued to accuse me of fraud. He said I’m so and so. “We want to shut this account down and  remove me from the lines.” She said fraud fraud fraud and said the account owner will have to go in a store. I then said I’m on the account. I’m paying the bill. I’d like to be removed. She said she was going to lock me out for a day bla bla bla fraud bla bla. She said You’re being recorded on a line! I said “Who cares if it’s recorded. Take me off the plan.” He said “Lady, we have been trying to shut this account down for days on end. None of the pin numbers you provide work for the account.” She kept saying fraud bla bla as some business move to keep people stuck on the line. So we hung up. Recalled just me and pops and the guy and I’m in front of the guy in the store and we were finally able to log in through his SS # and get the transfer number so I could leave t mobile to go to Spectrum carrier which is way better and easier to deal with. Left. Get there at the new place. T mobile still had the phone locked. Eventually called t mobile and were finally allowed to get the lock removed. Anything but T mobile and those other services. Spectrum is the best deal. Easy. Walk in. They aren’t ripping people off and they  are easy on the math. I got on a line. I was paying 200 bucks a month or something at T mobile. When I went to Spectrum, I got on the family plan for free as an added line. If not the family plan it’s like 30 bucks a month or something. Easy math. Great customer service. Spectrum is way better. They aren’t trying to rip you off with all those hidden confusing fees like T mobile or the other ones add on.

Zero, zero customer service, weak internet. Constantly having issue, my account some how is totally messed up. With another number therefore I can't do much with the account to get help. Just today I was told I didn't have an internet account that existed nor a Gateway that I had from T-mobile. My account was deleted some how  yet my account is being debit for payment monthly. I was told I would get a phone call an never received a call back.  After I was told to turn off the wifi  an not turn back on til I received a call from her/ representative.  Apparently she forgot its been over an hour. Every time I have had to call T mobile it's been a problem with the account number, pin code, etc I have been provided through T mobile and neither will allow me to get into the account.  This company is the worst . I would never recommend this company to anyone.
Just finished at the store in Elk Grove Village. The CS rep Jordan, was awesome.  He handled several customers as the only employee. The time it took to help everyone was amazing. Thank you, Jordan.

Terrible Customer Service !!! 
When someone who is a good -great customer from the United States calls for help , they do not want to speak with an Indian with a FAKE Name BOB who can not even speak good English ! This is VERY BAD business ! I promise you that Jatin or Rajiv in India does not speak to someone in America when they call from India , so why are we speaking to them and not someone in America ! 
I am offended and it is a waste of my time . The fact is that what little money they save by hiring an Indian or Filipino is nothing compared to what TMobile loses in Branding and Customer Satisfaction ! 
Stop putting US Customers over to foreigners who can not speak English properly , or we will all go back to your competition , Verizon .
Lousy service in store, lying bored staff. Phone customer service was good. Forget the stores. Seattle store appalling service in store.

I would give t mobile a -10 stars if I could.  I have a old damaged phone that I'm trying to get important data off of and they shut off my current services and tried to bill me $800 for a new phone that my insurance covered when all I wanted was to get my old fixed. No sympathy and horrible customer service. NEVER COMING BACK.

I wouldn't recommend tmobile to anyone after the mess I went through. I was told if I switched lines that they would pay up to 800 off of the lines I had with my previous carrier. They lied  and I now have a bill in collections. When I called to talk to someone at tmobile they told me I signed up for a different promotion. Why would I sign up for a promotion to not pay off all my phones ? This was not what I was told at all or I would have never signed up with them. So I'm left to pay on a phone that they have. Never again.

If you have senior parents BEWARE. They screwed my mother and father over something fierce. Their cel service stop working where they lived after 16 years and Tmobile convinced them they needed new phone. Not once, but twice. As I'm sure you have guessed it didn't fix any thing except putting my parents in debt. Bunch of crooks taking advantage of seniors. Did nothing to correct the situation. I hope I live to see they day they go bankrupt.

I rate as 1 because it doesn't allow me to rank 0 this company is a bunch of scammers they lie to you about the things you will get if your swap companies; Long story short they offer to refund me the 6-month already paid off to the other company but after I swap to their company they don't know anything about the refund they already offer me because isn't info in the system.
TOO BAD T-MOBILE LIE TO THE CUSTOMER WITHOUT REASON JUST I LOST 6 MONTHS OF SERVICE WITH MY OLDER COMPANY JUST FOR YOUR LIES

I needed a new phone and had been with Metro for 8 years. No complaints with Metro. Great price, great service. Rarely had any issue. I recently switched to T Mobile and also signed up for their internet. I'm not impressed with either. When I wake up and come home from work, I have to hard restart my TV every time. I constantly lose wifi, and half the time when the wifi drops out and my phone switches to 5G, it won't work. I'm really disappointed with this because since having a cell phone starting in 1998 (been with Airtouch/Verizon, Nextel, back to Verizon, to Metro, and now T Mobile) I have never had this bad of service.  I got a nice deal when I signed up. Free Samsung Galaxy s23, got the gateway for free. What is so odd to me is I had better service with their lower brand on a 4 year old phone than I do with Magenta Max and a new Galaxy. I can be a very loyal customer as long as I'm getting what I pay for. I'll give it a couple of more weeks, but I won't promise anything.
Zero stars.  Horrible company!  I cannot recommend using them.

I would say zero stars, but it doesn’t allow me to say zero. 
Our router and four phones arrived in the mail, after following the instructions, and not being able to connect the router we called customer service. Customer service, could not connect the router remotely and suggested we take it to the nearest T-Mobile store and exchange it. We went to the nearest T-Mobile store and they said they couldn’t help us because we didn’t buy it at the store so we went back home and called customer service.  Our name was spelled wrong on our account, and for security reasons, they refused to help us. At this point, we decided it wasn’t worth it and we decided to return the router and the phones and keep Verizon. Customer service gave us yet another phone number to call to return the router and the phones. Upon calling that number customer service again refused to help us because our name was spelled wrong on our account.  They said they couldn’t fix it and to go to our nearest store. This time we found the nearest store with a manager on duty and went to return the router and the phones. The manager called a phone line into T-Mobile to discuss the matter, and then finally we were able to return the router and the phones.  The manager told us we would get a bill, but not to pay it because the following month it would become void and we would receive a 0.00 bill. We in fact got a bill for $67.67. Keep in mind we never connected the router or the phones the phones were never taken out of the boxes. The router was never connected. Customer service couldn’t even connect the router and told us to return it. So, here we are after about three days of going back-and-forth between T mobile stores and our home trying to return a router and phones that were never used. We thought the nightmare was over. We in fact did get a bill like the manager said for $67.67 and we didn’t pay it like they said. But, the following month instead of getting a bill with a zero balance we got a collection notice from a collection agency.  we went back into a store to talk to a manager with the t mobile bills and paperwork. The manager said they couldn’t find a bill for $67.67 although the bill was sitting right in front of them and they acknowledged it was a legitimate bill. They couldn’t find where we owed the money or that it had been sent to collections, although we had The paperwork right in front of them. At this point the manager said “the computer shows you owe 40.00 and there is nothing on your account saying you were sent a bill for 67.67 or that it went to collections.”  Keep in mind the 67.67  bill is sitting on the counter in front of the manager and he as acknowledged it is a legitimate T mobile bill.  He states “ I don’t know where that bill came from or where or how it got sent to collections.”  Not even when he called  into T-Mobile could they figure it out. How bizarre! So, we said “what’s the $40 for?”  They said “because it took you so long to return the product.”  We said “we returned the product in two days.”  They said “that’s not what our records show.” So we paid the $40.  Bottom line here, essentially T-Mobile stole $40 from us.  And, we still have a bill from them in the amount of 67.67 which they sent to a collections agency we still have to deal with to which they’re only advice is to continue calling numbers and continue calling billing numbers to figure it out. This has been a nightmare and I’m sick and tired of T-Mobile. I wish I had never let them into my life. Stressful to the max and taking up valuable days of my life. Below it asks for the date of the experience and I can only enter one date but so far lasted for three weeks and it’s not over yet. It’s a nightmare do not buy anything or subscribe to T-Mobile in anyway whatsoever.

I have never experienced such blatant unprofessional tactics by a company in my life. My bill was past due as I don’t have money right now. All the places that would be hiring me for work opportunities since I’m free lance have my T-Mobile number. I just received a government free phone and called Tmobile to get a transfer pin so I could use my number. I explained to them the situation. They said they had to do a verification and send me a text message but my service was off they shut it off why would sending me a text message be the only way they could verify me. Anyways next day I go into the store following the directions of service rep I spoke with that day and sure enough the person In store says my account was closed yesterday and the only way to get my number would be to pay pay bill in full. I couldn’t believe it. I lost my mind and I’m sick. This company is such trash.

T.mobil 10800 Alpharetta Hwy Ste 304, Roswell, GA 30076
The person who seems to be the manager jumped in and interrupted the conversation between my wife and his staff. She was there to do a warranty exchange for the watches which my kids use. Our son who is in 3rd grade was along. He refused to do the exchange which T.mobil had already sent to his store saying that the devices were too scratched to be exchanged which is not the reality. When my wife expressed unhappiness he told her "With that attitude I am not going to help you". Later when I called him, he became threatening and told me that he will have me charged for tress pass. I told him that he is our nearest store and that I would ask him to bury the hatchet and let coolness prevail as my wife was having a hard day. I asked him why he treated my 5ft tall wife accompanied by our 3rd grader like an armed attacker to which he said that he has the whole episode recorded and that he will be notifying .... not sure who. Avoid this store.

T-Mobile is about as bad as they come as a big, bullying business. Here is a response to T-Mobile's rejection of my claim on the BBB. The BBB has not accepted that rejection. 

This is a letter rejecting a response T-Mobile made to the BBB. I purchased insurance originally for a 6 Pro that they failed to record on the phone. They freely admit this.

I am rejecting this response because:

T-Mobile states the following: "T-Mobile records indicate on March 16, 2023, Jimmy Dunn took advantage of our Equipment Installment Plan (EIP) with the purchase of a Google Pixel 6A handset. EIP is a payment option that allows eligible customers to purchase handsets by remitting a down payment at the time of purchase, if required, and agreeing to pay the remaining balance in monthly installments. 
Jimmy Dunn was not required to remit a down payment; however, they agreed to pay for the taxes on the full retail price. Jimmy Dunn then agreed to a series of 24 monthly installments for $18.75.
Please note, a new line of service ending in 3400 was added on March 16, 2023, updating the total number of lines to three. Further, the lines ending in 9850 were enrolled in our Protection with a monthly cost of $13.00"

The Pixel 6a was offered to me by the territory manager because it was going to take some time to get the 6 Pro covered. As stated in their letter, they originally told me that they would get the 6 pro covered. They said, "T-Mobile records reflect on September 11, 2022, Jimmy Dunn purchased a Google Pixel 6 Pro handset at Full Retail Price (FRP). Please note, at the time of purchase Jimmy Dunn’s phone number ending in 9850 was subscribed to our Protection insurance feature; however on September 30, 2022, due to an inadvertent error, the feature was removed.

On March 15, 2023, Jimmy Dunn contacted our Customer Care to receive information on how to process an insurance claim. Our Customer Care educated Jimmy Dunn that the line of service ending in 9850 was not enrolled in our Protection feature. At this time our Customer Care attempted to re-enroll the phone number ending in 9850 to our Protection feature. Please note, this feature is only eligible to be added during the first 30 days of purchasing a new device and/or during a qualifying event."

I had asked for coverage for the phone and was not supplied with the coverage, though I only found this out when the phone was damaged.
They offered the 6a to me to tide me over and stated that once my 6 pro was received by me, the 3rd line could be canceled. I had no desire to have, or be charged for a third line. I understood that, through their own mistake, the insurance coverage was not applied to my phone, but I also understood that they were going to make it right. They did not. Not only did I not get my 6 Pro replaced, but my phone bill also went up because of the third line. I was told by their representative that this would not happen. That I would get the 6 Pro and then cancel the third line.
Since then, the situation has been considerably aggravated.

About two weeks ago, I received a call from a representative named Berry with T-Mobile's Executive response, saying that he would like to correct the issue reported to the BBB. His offer was simple. He said that he would provide a 7 pro as a replacement for my 6 pro. He said that a charge for that would come through my account, but then he would issue a credit for that amount, as well as a credit of the amount for the third line, and issue a return for the 6a. I thought that was a good response, though all I ever asked was for a replacement for my 6a.

Without me ordering or signing any statement, the 7 Pro was indeed sent to me. I have never signed anything for the 7 Pro. I expected him to then provide the credit and the charge that was made against my bill would be removed, along with the charge for the third line.

After all that, I received nothing from Berry or Executive Response. Now I have a charge on my T-Mobile bill for about $1000 and I cannot get Berry to respond to me about the credit that he promised me. His telephone number, BTW, is 426-403-1471.
I am a retired individual who has just been diagnosed with possible kidney cancer. These charges are killing me. They are unfair, and it seems T-Mobile is conducting itself in an absolutely cutthroat manner. I presume that Berry, even though he sent me the new 7 Pro and placed a charge on my T-Mobile bill, has ceased communicating with me because T-Mobile expected to settle this claim with no action. But they have acted most unfaithfully.

Date of experience: September 11, 2022

After trying to resolve my issue with a T-Mobile store, I decided to log into my account to try and resolve my problem. They were as pitiful as their store. Same "bait and switch" promising one rate for internet and receiving another. Billy then tried to sell me on a different plan that would cost more. I ended the call before I lost my house or life savings.
No one give a _#&&$ about your issues. Hate this company.
I had the best agent. Christopher from Carribeans. He was absolutely super helpful.  Thanks Christopher.

I can't begin to explain what a horrible experience I had since TMobile merged with Sprint. Prior to the merger, I had been a happy Sprint customer for 8 yrs. Now, I'm stuck with 5 more months on a 2 yr contract with TM, which is a hellish experience every day!
The problems started with GPS and clock problems on my phone. It took 3 visits to TM stores before someone thought to offer to replace the existing Sprint SIM card. Relieved that the clockGPS issue was fixed, I was totally unprepared for the more than year-long issues with dropped calls, slow email & VM delivery, plus frequent phone shut off and restarts.
The worst experiences have been the situations with the other 2 phones on my acct. 
In Oct.2021, I bought 3 Galaxy S21 phones with insurance from TMobile. 
Now, One phone was lost in Jan.2022. I notified TM of the loss, only to be told that that phone was not insured! 3 different CSR's told me the same story. I had recently moved to another state,  so I didn't have the printed Assurant insurance information at hand. I just found the printed insurance letters a few weeks ago among some other legal documents. I called TM to let them know we needed to make some adjustments because the missing was insured.
Now, I'm being told that even though I had paid for the lost phone + service since Nov.2021, and I have the printed document to prove I insure all 3 phones when I ordered them, well....they have no notation of it in their system, so there's nothing that can be done but for me to continue to pay for it for the remained of the contract.
The 3rd phone on my acct. was used by a family member that depended on it to perform their job as a plumber. When the phone was 5 months old, the GPS started to malfunction, and the phone would shut off and restart on its own, usually before he reached his destination. TM replaced the phone the 1st time, but within a few days, the same problems started AGAIN! The next time he went to the same TM store and was talked into accepting a Samsung watch while the phone was being replaced for the 2nd time so he could navigate to his customer calls. Even a Supervisor confirmed that he could return the watch when his phone came in a few days and never mentioned that accepting the watch would extend the contract length, nor that the promised $5/mo service fee for the watch was all a big, fat lie! He went into the same store on 2 occasions to check on the delivery of the 2nd replacement phone and each time was told a notification had been sent (another lie!) for pickup, but he didn't come in to pick it up quickly enough, so the phone had been returned to the shipper! Now, we're stuck paying $10.37/mo. service fees for a watch that can not be used because he bought another model phone incompatible with the watch, and had it activated on the old TM ph.#. And, 2 wks ago, Assurant sent a letter stating that the insurance on the malfunctioning phone was scheduled to end at the end of April 2023!!! When I called to inquire as to why the insurance was ending, I was told that the insurance was automatically transferred to the newly activated phone. Then I was transferred to a Sprint CSR, who told me that the insurance didn't exist on either phone that was associated with that line!
TMobile is the worst company I have ever done business with in my life! They are the worst liars and thieves. They exist to grift their customers out of as much money as possible. TM couldn't care less about providing decent cell phone service or functional equipment. They expect your payments on time every month regardless of how they have insulted your intelligence, lied to you, hung up o you during CS calls, nor how many hours of your time you have spent on the phone trying to get some modicum of satisfaction from the money you are spending for the crappy cell service or the 4th rate equipment.
If you're not under contract with TM now, for God's sake, don't make the biggest mistake of the decade by entering into any contract with them. You will regret it for a very long time!

Next to no one can comprehend English and 9 out 10 are totally incompetent. They stole 4 very frustrating hours of my life trying to accomplish very simple tasks. Will NEVER do business with them again!!!

First issue is that they began my billing cycle the same day they shipped the sim, even though all available information clearly stated that billing would not begin until I activated the sim, which wasn't until over ten days after it was shipped. 
Second issue was TERRIBLE data speeds, which on my plan, was what the majority of the price was based on. I was paying about 50 dollars MORE than a basic voice/data plan as I use a good amount of data monthly and selected a plan to accommodate that. My speeds ranged most often from 2-3 kiloBITS to 30 kiloBITS (im capitalizing there to make clear that im NOT referring to kiloBYTES) if I was LUCKY. So slow that text only emails were timing out before they were able to even fully load, and web pages? Forget about it! Every single webpage I tried to load timed out with error messages.
When I contacted them to see what was going on they did the usual 0 accountability thing where they do their best to convince you that it's your phone that's the issue, not their crappy service. Then when my phone was ruled out as the culprit, they tried to convince me that THOSE ABYSMAL DATA SPEEDS WERE AVERAGE AND STANDARD DATA TRANSFER SPEEDS! (Verizon averages 2-3 MEGABYTES/S and sometimes better, and ATT averages around 5 MEGABYTES and often even reaches as high as 8.5 for example. Tmobiles nonsense that they try to say is "standard/average" was literally hundreds of times slower)

At that point when I realized the convo was going to accomplish nothing because this guy was just going to assume I knew nothing about tech and try to lie through his teeth to portray their junk as industry standards even though my service was Far from any standard or average, I told them to cancel my service. 
And they sure did!!! Suddenly they summoned all the previously lacking competence and in the blink of an eye, got right on that! Of course they did....  Only instead of leaving my Sim active through the end of the billing cycle as their policy states, THEY DEACTIVATED MY SIM WITHIN TEN MINUTES, adding insult to injury after this crap experience, unexpectedly and with no warning, leaving me with no voice service!
So..
 I assumed there would be no bill since THEY deactivated it prior to the end of my billing cycle and it was only activated for less than 72 hours.
WRONG!   
JUST TODAY, I see that they billed me the full month!!!!
I will be filing a charge back with my payment processor as well as filing complaints with the FCC and my states C.P.B over their fraudulent billing practices as well as their clearly intentional refusal to abide by their own clearly stated policies regarding billing cycles, start and stop time frames, and cancellation timelines.
Stay away from these shady slimeballs at all costs!

If I could give a negative star I would.  I really hate to even give 1 star.  I had the worst experience with T-Mobile.  I have absolutely no confidence if their service at this point.  I have spent hours on the phone with their people and apparently they don't put notes in when you call because they keep passing you to another person and no one knows what's going on..

I called on tue. to transfer my service to t-m and thur night the had sent the sims cards.  the sales person didn't bother to tell me i would need to pay of my device first.  so i called for help when my phone wouldn't work,  spent all day on the phone.  next thing you know my phone stopped working.  t-m had canceled my phone number with att that i had for years.  then they told me to go to the store.  The guy at Siloam Springs AR t-m store was pretty rude, didn't offer to even look at my account, he just said I can't help you.  I would go talk to att.  so i went back to att and told them to just reactivate my number and they said they could not, it was cancel due to whatever t-m did.  so now i had to get a new phone. and they phone I had now I will get to pay this balance out of my pocket.  So just a word of advice, do not go with T-moble, they don't know their butt from a hole in the ground.  very disorganized and will tell you one thing and not follow through.  I hope this will help others to not leave a good mobile company with the promise of a lower bill.  It will cost you much more in the end.

I was with sprint for 14 years and never had a problem with upgrades until T-Mobile took over and now the rules have changed with no notifications what so ever and now I am having to go thru a lot of problems to get an upgrade for a phone which over the years I have never had this happen before so if you want Sprint/T-Mobile for your carrier please choose another company unless you want to go thru the hassle I have been going thru the past 4 months still with no satisfaction
Lorien Rodrigos Was the best representative that I’ve talked to and she’s very nice and she’s really funny she made my experience with customer service very nice. She’s the best.

Bad service, If you call on a weekend when their not in normal business hours the transfer you to a foreigner which you can barley understand and really can't help you. I just spent over a hour and half on the phone with an agent from India who lied to me multiple times and then charged me at the end. I have been trying to add a second line to tomobile for the past 3 days and each agent I talk to gives me different info so no one is communicating with anyone each one acts like their brand  new. I thought this  was different from ATT but same bull different provider

I recently purchased a phone and plan at T-mobile in Alabaster Alabama. Benny Alvarado was awesome. He was so helpful and very nice. I was originally told they didn’t have the phone I wanted so I reluctantly chose another phone I didn’t like as well since I didn’t have time to wait on a phone to be shipped. Benny went in the back and found one last phone of my choice hidden in the back. He had so much patience when I couldn’t remember my different passwords and had to restart set up twice. He was kind and very courteous during the whole process. I love my service, plan and internet. I would highly recommend this company and this employee
Worse experience ever worse customer service ever worse experience ever !!!!!!!

Absolutely the worst. The service and customer service is embarrassing. I was told I would have great service lots of towers around but after I switched I had nothing! I spent hours on the phone, when I could get through, due to no service, and NEVER got a resolution! Don't believe the hype, it is not worth it! Totally a disgrace!

I have been a T. mobile customer for more than 15 years and pay my bills on time. Last month I was late in paying my phone bill by one day and they charged me a late fee. Today,I called customer service and requested that since this is the first time the late fee be waived. He simply gave me a long lecture on how I can avoid paying late fee in the future but did not waive the late fee. These people have no regard for their loyal customers. No wonder they have such bad reviews.

I placed an order on 04/14/2023 expecting to be able to pick up the package from a UPS location. When I called T-Mobile customer service they acknowledged that they charged a closed account and said they would refund the money and I would receive the refund on 05/21/2023. Dealing with them has been a complete nightmare and their customer service is completely incompetent.

This is the worst cable company ever, and the customer service is horrible. I’m quitting T-Mobile which will cost them more then the $1000 promotion (they will not honor!) due to the lost of revenue they would have received in the remaining 18 months I would have been paying them, much less the years I would have been a loyal customer. I’m stunned by this blatantly poor business decision.  I am writing negative revues everywhere I can, and at least 2 friends that were going to switch are now going elsewhere. If you are thinking about joining service with Tmobile, make sure you run away. Their service is the worst, and when you call them to complain, they'll tell you they are fixing cell towers. Then when you decide to cancel service with them, they search for every charge on the account and bill you claiming you owe all these fees and device fees. Please spread the word, we cannot allow others to experience this. LET'S FADE T-MOBILE OUT!!!

I was with Sprint for years and when T-Mobile bought them out I was forced to become a T-Mobile customer.  For almost the first year things were OK until I bought new phones for the six lines on my account.  Those phones came with deal like half off with a trade in and things of that nature.  EVERY bill since the purchase of the new phones did not include the discount.  So I had to call customer service, which is obviously outside of the US and explain my problem every single month.  And every single month the representative told me the problem was solved and it would not happen again.  However, it continued to happen.  As a result, I paid off all my phones immediately after the fifth month of this occurring and signed up with another carrier.  I do not recommend T-Mobile at all, their customer service is not really helpful at all and they don't honor  deals that were applied when purchasing new phones.

TMobile has terrible customer service, both on line or in its few remaining stores as it has been closing many stores every week. Calls get crossed, calls drop, reception is so poor that I much prefer my GV!
Online assistance circles endlessly through non workable options. Once I spoke with an overseas rep who kept me on the line for 35+ minutes while contradicting basic account info, making me hang up! At any time, stores have at least half a dozen customers waiting for a staff of two. TMobile has kept open its worst store in west Los Angeles while closing its better locations such as Beverly Hills where I got great efficient service without added Los Angeles fees.
I am looking to change my cell service asap.

Ive been in the store # 8637 at Dania Beach, N Miami to ask for roaming to use in Europe. ENDER, the representative told me the cost will be $ 15 extra and he activate it. 
I wasnt really convinced and 3 days after I called the company and Ive been told the activation was just for long distance calls. They dont care about customers needs and many of them dont know what they offer!
I RECOMEND TO ASK OR CALL TO DIFFERENT STORES AND MAKE 100% SURE ON WHAT YOU ARE PAYING FOR BECAUSE NOT EVERYBODY TAKE SERIOUS WHAT THEY DO.

T-Mobile had a breach a few years ago. My phone number was sold on the Dark Web. Now two to three times a day I have all of these strange numbers calling my phone as far as from New Mexico. It is annoying and very frustrating because I do not really talk on the phone. I do not want to change my phone number of 12 years.
I dont have this issue when i was in sprint .Their service is so terrible. Once you are inside the building not be able to call or receive a call. Just waiting to be off the contract.

Wanted to get better service had MetroPCS thought going to T-Mobile would be better but it was worse. The first month we realized we made a mistake but for some reason we stayed for a whole year which was also a mistake canceled the service come to find out we were still getting bills. Was signed up for another line that we never even used had no usage on it for a whole year had to fight to get the money back. Got that taken care of but when we went in there they told us that the phones were free if we used our old phone number that we already had so we said okay the manager there was corrupt at that time she was signing us up for other services without even telling us come to find out that we were in a contract she used autofill to sign in a signature that wasn't ours to sign us up for a contract because she was trying to get commission off of everything that we purchased the autofill is regular handwriting not cursive so make sure you look for that if you were to go get a phone through T-Mobile so you know that you're not signed up with a contract you didn't even know you had Make sure you look for extra services also was told by multiple employees that there are employees at all the places here in Medford Oregon that are adding things on without telling you so they can get commission off of it. I was on the phone with customer service for 3 days and results from 504 for the phones ended up having to pay $55 for the phones even though the contract  was not a actual contract cuz we never signed it without our consent they did the auto filter. Found out that the manager has done this with multiple people and she was fired for it but also found out that other people do that there too. I would not recommend T-Mobile some of their employees are very nice but some of them will scam you and you won't even know it until you cancel with them. Please be careful if you decide to go T-Mobile would not recommend them to anybody.

They are very nice when sell you the service, not that nice, when you are leaving. Make sure you're switching to another provider at the END of your billing cycle. They DON'T  PRORATE your last bill.
Plus they lost our personal data. 
NEVER again!
T mobile app sucks always down unless it time to pay the bill and then it's working fine....

I called in about charges to my account and the rep Edwin was unwilling to help correct what the sales representative (#****553) at Store# 1CSI. I was told that the iPhone 14+ included the liquid screen protector in the price. This was a lie, you are charging me $4.17/mo + the phone cost. The same rep also "waived" the $35 activation fee, which was a lie per my receipt. This is fraudulent inducement & deceptive trade practices! They also sent me home with an internet device that was supposed to have a 30d trial period, nope, same rep gave false information. It was only a 15d trial period and they attempted to charge me for that as well until the store returned the device to THEIR warehouse! Mane T-Mobile really sucks bruh they talk all that smack saying are phone service is the best he’ll no it’s not they always blame it on the tower’s saying they are fixing the towers for the last 3 months my phone has been on 1 bar I’m finna switch carriers

Dog sh** customer service. Messed up a new device and won't let me unlock it despite being paid off. Doesn't take accountability for mistakes of the company. No way any other company could be worse than this
Horrible company, very fraudulent in their practices. There customer service is non existent.

Was told would get 2 phones and lines free for $27.50 each. No other charges were mentioned.  It was a trial for 10 days - if returned within 10 days only fee would be a $40 restock fee. The employ finished the paperwork, folding it and placing it into the bag with phones then told me there was an activation fee of $35 for each phone- I paid it along with an additional fee of 41.94 tax on the phone. After getting home (this phone was for my mother) we looked at the paperwork and it indicated the phones would be billed monthly at $19each for 24 months and there was be a $65 month fee for not using auto pay. I called immediately and she told me- "don't worry, we have to write it up that way but your bill will show the correct figures. My 10 days were up May 10 but bill didn't come out until the 17th. We did this on April 27, 2023.  Went back next day & returned everything- she wanted to know why and I told her bc she had electronically signed my mothers name to all the papers and I don't do business that way. Told her I wanted my refund less the $40-  she said we will credit your card $2.50- they applied $40 to the tax for the phones and charged me for activation.  When I questioned her about this and reminded her what she said- she refused to speak to me. DO NOT USE T  MOBILE.   THEY ARE DISHONEST and do not give you all the information - if I had waited for the bill , I would have been charged for all the additional items.  So, I paid $70 for nothing.  There are more reputable companies out there.  T Mobile is not one of them in Dothan AL.

This is the worst company I have ever dealt with. I have 3 phones and internet services. Every location you go to has different policies. The managers lie to you and make promises that they cannot keep. Example: I saw a manager at the Melbourne mall, "You will get $800 on trade in, and your bill will be increased by $24. I didn't have all the phones with me, so I went back 2 days later, it was then a $500 trade in, and the bill would increase by $110. The manager was not there, so I said I will come back. I later went to the Malabar branch, and was told that I had to pay $35 upgrade fee and taxes on $3,200 for the phones. They were busy, so I left. Two days later, I went back to the same location and was told I will get a $300 trade in credit, unless I updrade to a different plan, only then I will get the $800. That was never mentioned in the first meeting with the manager. He was belligerent, lacked decorum, conversing with staff about me in front of me. This man should be fired, but Tmobile reputation has plummeted since I first received service, so they hire the scumbags that keep railroading their customers. I will be filing a formal complaint with the corporate office, not that it will make any difference. A word of wisdom: Other companies are giving the 23+ phone for free!
After 38 calls no one was able to help.  Thanks alot

Minus 2
It's disappointing that you must give at least one star to write the review, when you really deserve a minus two. I'm escalating my complaint to a state representatives office because T-Mobile won't cancel a bill. I've tried the phone(twice), I visited the store to verify who I am, and still two months later I'm still receiving a bill. 

I hope everyone reading this will also send a notice to their congressional representative as I think T-Mobile is using us to pad profit numbers before quarterly reports are due...an illegal practice. 

It's absurd, but your state representative lives in your area and would love to uncover such a scandal. This is what they do and they are able to help. If you've had a billing problem with T-Mobile, please reach out to your representative's office to discuss the issue (It's what they are there for).

I was with t mobile for seven years and finally left because the bill kept increasing. When I switched to Verizon, I received a statement for $358. I immediately called to speak with a supervisor, and he told me I was a month behind. I told him that could not have been possible because my phone would have been turned off. Then the "supervisor" says, "Either you pay the bill, or we will put it on your credit." Needless to say, they are a rip-off, and the customer service is disgraceful. The "supervisor" could not explain how their business came up with such a figure. The sad part is that my mother warned me about t mobile because years ago, the company slapped an extra bill on her when she switched to another carrier. Save yourself the money and time to not deal with this business nonsense. T- mobile is a liar and complete scam artist.
Unbelievably bad service. If you looked for worse service you could not gind one. Awful awful awful. There are jo words how bad they are. STAY AWAY FROM T MOBILE

The service is fine, the internet is fast, but the problem are the dishonesty of the people that work there. They charged me for 2 hot spots telling me at first that it was free. And when I called to cancel it, they charged me to cancel the lines. And a friend of mine got insurance without asking for it, and he wasn't even aware of it. Now he is fighting someone on the phone asking to cancel it, and I really hope that they don't charge him for no reason.

I have been a T-Mobile customer on a family plan for 22 years. The bill for my family plan currently is about $230, including tax. I asked simply that the bill be transferred to my daughter. Harold said (a) it would be difficult since it is tied to my social security number, then said (b) he could do it, but that it would take another billing cycle, then... (c)...going through the bill, it would be much more. I told him that switching the payer of the bill should not incur additional charges and that it was unacceptable.  I read this back to him.  He agreed. After speaking with his supervisor, Harold confirmed to me that the name would change, but that there would be NO additional charges.  I told him that I would get my daughter on the line and call back, getting an assurance that there were notes in the file.  I also RECORDED the conversation.  I was also told that this assurance would be in the notes.

When I called T-Mobile back with my daughter on the line, we were given the runaround for 50 minutes by Rod (employee #5998002) and her supervisor Esa (employee #2025720). When pressed, both said they could not do this. 

This is both disrespectful to a long-standing customer and an indication of T-Mobile’s greed. We will change to a different provider.

Does anyone at that company know what is going on with the rebates? I've spent over 2 hours on the phone with some script reading peons that all they know how to do is apologize. Not a one had a solution to my painless switch over from my previous carrier, to whom I am returning to just as soon as they live up to their word. Do not believe any salesman for they cannot live up to their promises.

ABSOLUTELY PIECE OF GARBAGE!!!
The Internet CONNECTION CONSTANTLY DROPS
5G- TOTAL BULL GARBAGE 
Just last night talked to my brother, in California using Whatsapp, TOTAL FAILURE. Video is blurry, sound very poor.
Had to use my other line with cricket. Way better!!!
TMobile, should be renamed to Gmobile. G stands for GARBAGE!!!
I wish I had POWER to punish them STARTING FROM CEO!!!!

For being a loyal customer for over 20yrs the way thay treat you. They offer new customer better promos and offers than to us and expect that bs bubble gum t-mobile Tuesday for customer appreciation. Cheap ass. Not even a dam thank you but they quickly to keep on changing you for services you don't have

I changed from Verizon because they made an issue when I wanted to change my phone number. Took a few days, I got fed up and looked online and found t-mobile. The selling point was that they would pay off my phone, plus I thought the service was a little better.  I got transferred to t-mobile and then tried to get the reimbursement for the phone. I needed my Verizon account number and PIN which I could not recall. So I called Verizon and was informed that I would need to go to one of the corporate offices and get a PIN renewed. Waited about an hour for service and was given a temporary PIN. I called back to t-mobile and they informed me the PIN would not work because of the phone number change, and that it was a different kind of PIN. I didn’t know there were different PIN numbers evidently. I explained to Verizon the PIN was needed by t-mobile so they would pay off the phone, they informed me the Pin I was given would work. The lady at t-mobile stated it would not. T-mobile sent me the link anyway to complete the required information, I tried anyway and got an error message that I was already a T-mobile customer and not eligible for the phone payoff option. So once again I called back to T-mobile and explained and was informed I would need to pay off the phone first then they would reimburse. I got a different story every time I called T-mobile. I gave up, too many phone calls, conflicting messages and overall BS. So now I am stuck with T-mobile, no financial advantage of changing carriers, and had to pay off the balance due on the phone myself when I cancelled Verizon. Overall customer service SUCKS everywhere. They offer these bogus promotions but really do not want to pay anyone.

My son is deployed on active duty in Australia and they lie their ass off about they're going to call you back and what they're going to do they say they're the first and last time you'll ever have to call about the problem but they never call back to follow up as they promise if you're on active duty make sure that you get everybody's ID otherwise they'll lie their ass off like this person

Make sure you keep the ID this is a supervisor that's been running me around for the last four weeks they lie their ass off never keep their word and lie about what's in the notes

I was a Sprint customer for years. It wasn't perfect, but nothing crazy. Once Tmobile came in, it was hell from day 1. Customer service was impossible to reach, and when you could they would just straight up lie to you to get rid of you. They all have fake names and answer questions like bots. It took 2 weeks to activate a new phone because they'd tell me, "just wait 24 hours" over and over.
I tried to be patient, hoping it was just a rough adjustment period. That was years ago. I am finally switching to a new carrier, and they refuse to unlock my (fully paid off) phone.
I was just hung up on after sitting on hold for 25 minutes, because the chat told me (you'll never guess) I need to wait 24 hours for my phone to magically fix itself.

If you have any self respect, you'd be better off tying two tin cans together than subjecting yourself to this cesspool if a company.
Tmobile has too many departments. One does not know what the other is doing. Worst phone service  ever.I hate them.

Be very cautious about online chat to obtain new cellular service through T-Mobile. Agents do not know their product lines and give misleading information regarding values for trade in devices. After two and a half hours of chatting with an agent I asked when the value of my trade would be determined and she stated at the time they receive the pre-application.   I ended up filling out the application with  my personal information and  I submitted them asked what the value of my trade in devices came in at. At this point the lies unraveled. I informed her I had screen shots of my question and the answer she provided.  I asked for her direct supervisor.  She supplied me with customer service.  From there the run around trying to get to speak to a supervisor continued for another hour before they just placed me on a long hold and disconnected the call. With the ethics of the sales team and the lack of concern from all parties, customer service to sales to activation I would recommend using extreme caution when considering changing to this Carrier
Sorry to say T-Mobile customer service is the worst I have ever experienced.  They just don't care and LIE!  I recommend you find another carrier to service your needs

So sad! Two visits to two different TMOBILE stores in two months. Left the St. Helena Island store shaking our heads over the unfriendly customer representative. Decided not do business with that store. Ended up adding a line and phone online. 
Next visit to purchase another new phone we tried the Beaufort store. Wow! Three reps sitting on counter,  all on their phones.  Not one welcomed us. Not one was even interested in assisting us. We felt unwelcome and had to initiate the sale ourselves. Through the entire sale the representative never looked me in the eyes.  He and the other two reps made us feel unwelcome and uncomfortable. Kind of like we were disturbing them.  Kind of like they were getting paid by the hour and not commission and didn't want or need our money. Which either way is an unfortunate way to treat a customer of 18 years or a day.
In addition the store looked to not have been cleaned in weeks. The carpet was covered in debris, candy wrappers, plastic wrappings, potato chips and other unidentifiable stuff.  Looking into the office through an open door I could see a trash can overflowing onto the floor. 
From the beginning to the end a regrettable experience.  Made me sorry I've been doing business with them as long as I have been.

Where do I begin with this mess?! 
Well I’ve been given 6 different response when trying to find answers on receiving rebate. Basically when I switched my Internet, I was told that it would take 24 to 48 hours to receive my modem and with my modem they assign a phone number and with that phone number is what I used to then register my rebate, virtual card, and after I register my account, I will receive a email or text with my virtual card information but that clearly is not the case. It is 6 to 8 weeks when I called to check on it after the 48 hours I was told that I had to wait till the promotion ended and then that’s when they sending all the accounts to the third party and that’s where you will receive your  virtual card number. Will fast forward several more calls. I was told today that they don’t expedite it after I was told on my last call, they were expediting it not to mention on my last call they couldn’t find my rebate. I had to look up my email to verify it because they didn’t even see it in the system. They told me I had a $50 rebate and I didn’t think much of it. I just found the email verification and he verified it removed on today when I called because it’s not was not expedited. I was told that I not only had a $200 rebate that I knew about but there was a $50 rebate that I was supposed to receive as well but no one sent an email or a text message telling me to register for it so it expired and that’s post to be my problem. Clearly it take 6-8 weeks for them to process these cards because they’re waiting for you to make two payments. Now mind you the $50 rebate that I was never told about was supposed to cover my first months payment, which really doesn’t matter because my employer is paying my Internet as a benefit for working from home. So the conclusion of my phone call today is that when I mentioned all the negative reviews on customers, not receiving their virtual rebates, and t-Mobile, not honoring customers rebates I was told it’s because those are the customers that never register their card and I find that very hard to believe that every single one of the complaints that I’ve read is because the customers did not follow through their process and register their card. I just find that very hard to believe that that many people did not know they had to register their cards so I don’t believe that at all and my second conclusion is that I am never going to receive my rebate card because I am not going to pay my first two months before they decide they wanna send it out because that’s not what I agreed to and that’s not what I signed up for so they’re not getting a payment until I get my card because I am actually told as a selling point that that card for $200 rebate card could be used towards my first four payments first for monthly bills or for anything else or any other point to sell I wanted to use the card for, but it could go towards my first 4 months of service. so what I would like to know is how exactly would that work if they’re not planning on issuing the card until I’ve made two months worth of payments, but when signing up for the Internet or switching my Internet, I was told that the rebate card could be used towards my first four months of payments if I choose to. This is probably a promotion rebate that I will never see and I can understand where all of the complaints are coming from T-Mobile uses these rebates to get customers to change switch or starting new service, with no intention of actually sending out the rebates. I will end it. On this note I have had plenty of jobs in customer service and tech companies. I know how things operate and I know that there are things that people can do to speed things along. It’s one button you push so with that being said, if they really wanted to resolve the issue, they could as for the $50 rebate that I was aware of, and it expired with no mention to me even after speaking to six different people just makes me laugh and confirms that T- mobile just uses these promotions  to gain new customers and then not fulfilling the big switch over rebates.. it looks like this will be the shortest relationship I’ve ever had! Just a little background so that I can be clear I’m not one to jump around for free promotion, rebates whatever you would like to call them. I’ve had the same Internet since 2003. The only reason I had to shop around is because I needed faster service to work remotely and my offer that speed of Internet in my area. My cell phone carrier has also been the same since 2001. i’m not just being a Karen or service, jumping for bonuses, promotions or rebates, etc..

Thank you for reading 🙂

Worst customer service I have with T-Mobile with last 8 years bought a phone in BOGO and figured it out they still charging for this phone for over a year. I talked to the Customer representative they said they can’t do any thing.

I am dating this review today, but the issue has been ongoing ever since I started my account in January. Put simply, I have never been able to successfully get the seemingly all important Tmobile ID. I have a line number, an account number, and even a (useless) password. But nothing gets me an ID. I've been to four different stores and they were all willing to help, but unable to do so. Online application process is always disabled. You can call the Support Number (if you have an hour to waste) and then get your call dropped when you protest that you don't know what they mean by a "6 digit zipcode". No matter, when I finally found out they meant a pin number after talking to a store employee, it proved equally worthless. To date I still have no ID. The Hotspot works well enough, but I can't wait to finally get away from the need for one so I can ditch anything remotely connected to T-mobile. I thought Verizon and Comcast were bad, but this is on another level altogether!

I had decided to purchase home internet from these guys a while back and have had them. Up until just recently. See, when I first made the call to get the Internet, ( I rarely utilize this) but  I figured I would ask about veterans discount considering most places do honor some sort I believe. I was thanked for my service and was told yes and that I was now going to receive a XX% discount. About a year later I called about a bill question to basically find out I was lied to and have not gotten said discount for over a year. Lol yea definitely not staying with these clowns. That is not a very nice thing to do. I would have been cool with a no when I asked, but to be led on and think for so long I was? Wow shame on you T-Mobile.

Daniel at the Ferndale Washington location helped me out with some functionalities with the auto pay as we'd experienced some fraud issues with our last credit card...just overall helpful without attitude, I don't live on my phone it's still only a tool for business. Daniel and his colleague Andrew were awesome, friendly...no hassles, just great service!!!!

We have been waiting 6 days so far to just get a phone unlocked. We have gotten all kinds of promises about call backs and never received them. We needed one phone unlocked because son moved to an area wher t mobile does not work. And still nothing happened absolutely awful service.

I've been a customer for 12 years....Within this last year their customer service has gone to crap. Good luck speaking to someone stateside. The customer service experience is not the same. There is no sense of true concern for your issues. It feels as though I am speaking to robots only programmed to provide scripted responses. I'm constantly being lied to,the notations are incomplete, and My call times last 2-8hrs....I might as well be on payroll. Although it's not 100% of the reps, it's 80/20 as of late. I have 10 lines and I'm  willing to take them all to another company at this point. I hate to leave because I'm loyal but my loyalty is given, it's earned. They are pushing me away. I don't work hard for my money to pay someone to treat me this way.

The rep in the store lied to me. I bought a phone and she set it up and was very friendly. When I got home I found that I had no service. I called TMobile on my daughter's phone and was told that there's no coverage where I live. But, TMobile would not release my phone number, I had to return the phone and buy another phone with a new number. I'm still being charged for service that I have never had. I can't get any help from anyone.

edits.text: Worst customer service ever. Salespeople lied to me, sold me a phone that doesn't get service in my area. Locked my number so I had to change it. Charged me for another month of service and won't refund the money.

text: After canceling my account T-Mobile owed me nearly $78.  I have a paper billing statement attesting to this.  Customer service stated that because I didn't have an account any longer there "was nothing I can do."  This was despite being in an actual T-Mobile Store with an employee that verified we have a bill stating that we are owed the money.  We were put on hold multiple times and then asked for a supervisor.  He also stated that he could "do nothing" to get us the money since the account was closed.  We went back and forth for nearly an hour before we told them that we would contact an attorney is they insisted on not sending us what was owed.  It was only then that this supervisor decided he could send a "prepaid VISA" for the amount owed  (which incidentally proved the earlier lie about being able to "do nothing").

Elena at TMobile wrote the amounts of my contract.  When I got the bill it is $30 more a month than she wrote.   CS told me that the price offered was with a rate plan that cost $10 more a month.  The rep apologized repeatedly.  The supervisor gave me a $12 credit on the first bill, also aplolgizing repeatedly.  Yet no concern for not honoring the prices quoted me by the TMobile sales person.  Now I have noticed that TMobile website notes that my bill can change at any time.  The CS told me if I put a hold on my debit card that TMobile will charge me late fees and reactivation fees.  Seems TMobile legal has no concern for honest representation of charges.  TMobile will not honor the prices that their sales people present during signing.  This is total bait and switch.

I have been a Sprint/T-mobile customer for 18 years. I had been having issues with my phone, dropping calls, never had this problem before. Called spoke with T-mobile Customer Technical Support (Jasmine). She is amazing, she tried a few things and stated she would call me back later than evening to see if it worked. As promised she called me back, I explained to her that I was still having issues. She asked if I would take the phone to T-mobile so that they could change the sim card, she also explained additional options. Again, Jasmine stated that she would call me back in a day to see if it was resolved. As promised she called and I explained to her that I was still having issues. Jasmine then stated there's one more thing I want to try, and it actually worked. She was so professional, courteous, thoughtful, knowledgeable, and would not give up until my issue was resolved. Thank You Jasmine.

Our service was great for 5 weeks then it would cut out every 20 min for a week. I called was told they were working on the tower. A week later they still were working on it. I traded my modem at the tmobile store for a diff one only to be told "this one won't work any better" and it didn't. I called to warranty it on a Thurs and was told I would have it in 1 to 4 days (kind of broad don't you think?) In day 3 I called to get tracking for it and learned it hadn't even been shipped yet. It came the next day with an overnight label on it but it gave an error message of "no Sim card" so they sent me incomplete equipment. I tried to use the sim card out of the other one but it would not work. At this point I've been with no internet for 2 weeks and I was done with Tmobile. I returned both modems to the store only to be told I would have to take the one that was shipped to me to a UPS store -- even more inconvenience. I was never told that others were having issues. It was just me. Will never go back

CONTRACT IS UNREADABLE AND CANNOT DETERMINE MONTHLY PAYMENT UNTIL BILL ARRIVES. MUCH HIGHER THAN THEY SAID AT STORE, BUT TOO LATE TO RETURN ITEM. 

TOTAL RIP OFF.

I'm a US Citizen living part-time overseas. I opened a T-Mobile cell account, during the process, I was yelled at, treated rudely, long wait hold times, disconnected and hung up on several times.  I managed to finally complete the application with required identification,  and the service was activated.   As I'm currently in Australia,  I  used T-Mobile for two calls, and used the data when my Australian data was unavailable.   Next thing I know, I discovered that my service has been cancelled. No explanation.  No email.  No communication. No calls  Because an incompetent worker typed ny DOB wrong, it's been flagged as Fraud. No explanation.  NO COMMUNICATION.   Please, if you want customers, do your job.

Once I switched from Sprint to T-Mobile, my automatic payment started coming out anywhere from 2-3 days earlier than the actual due date. It has caused me such a headache. I really hate this deceptive practice.
